claim: Kimi K2 Thinking beats GPT-5 on Humanity's Last Exam (44.9 vs 41.7), an open-source reasoning model outperforming closed frontier models on hard benchmarks
actionable: For hard reasoning tasks (math, exams), test Kimi K2 Thinking; it's competitive with or better than GPT-5 on specific benchmarks.
source_title: Kimi K2 Thinking is CRAZY... (HUGE UPDATE)

claim: Kimi K2 Thinking scores 60.2% on Browse Comp (agentic search) vs 54.9% for GPT-5 and 24.1% for Claude Sonnet; massive advantage for agent browsing
actionable: For agent-based web search and reasoning, prefer Kimi K2 Thinking; it dominates agentic benchmarks.
source_title: Kimi K2 Thinking is CRAZY... (HUGE UPDATE)

claim: Kimi K2 Thinking can execute 200-300 sequential tool calls in a single chain of thought; search, reason, search again, adapting based on results
actionable: Use Kimi K2 Thinking for complex agentic tasks requiring many sequential tool calls; its thinking mode handles long-horizon planning.
